purpose: Explicit retrospective reconstruction of GP helper mappings for workflows 0626-0675; no new clinical research.

searchQueriesUsed: site:dha.gov.ae abdominal bloating primary care guideline UAE
site:nice.org.uk CG61 bloating distension bowel habit red flags investigations follow-up
site:nice.org.uk NG12 persistent abdominal distension bloating ovarian colorectal
candidateSourcesRejected: DHA abdominal-pain telehealth guidance used as complete evidence for isolated bloating
IBS management recommendations used to diagnose or treat undifferentiated bloating
ovarian-cancer symptom guidance applied without age, anatomy, persistence, or frequency qualifiers
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejectionReasons: Pain guidance does not cover isolated or chronic bloating as a complete presentation.
The workflow does not establish the adult duration or diagnostic criteria for IBS.
Cancer guidance is applicable only in recommendation-qualified contexts and cannot define the generic workflow.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
workflows.populationApplicability: Adults with recurrent abdominal bloating where the exact IBS symptom domains are relevant; acute illness, children, pregnancy, postoperative, pelvic, inflammatory, obstructive, malignant, and systemic contexts require separate evidence.
workflows.settingApplicability: Primary-care documentation and follow-up as qualified by the exact source and clinical context.
workflows.uaeApplicability: International evidence requires UAE primary-care, laboratory, prescribing, referral, safeguarding, and local-pathway adaptation.
workflows.recencyVerification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
workflows.supersededCheck: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.
unresolvedSourceGaps: Acute onset, pregnancy, pelvic symptoms, visible distension, examination, ovarian context, obstruction, ascites, medicine, diet, exact tests, diagnosis, referral, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejectionReasons: The workflow does not calculate or contain all ten AUDIT responses.

Alcohol documentation does not establish liver disease.

Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
workflows.populationApplicability: Adults in primary care where alcohol screening or brief assessment is appropriate; adolescents, pregnancy, intoxication, withdrawal, acute injury, and dependence treatment require separate evidence.

workflows.recencyVerification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
workflows.supersededCheck: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.
unresolvedSourceGaps: Exact beverage, quantity, frequency, timing, standard-drink conversion, AUDIT responses, score, pregnancy, withdrawal, diagnosis, counselling, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
